name: United States
intro: In the United States, physical gold is classified as a collectible, resulting in a federal tax rate on the gain of up to 28%, distinct from usual stock rates.
overview: There is no federal VAT in the United States, but some states apply a sales tax depending on the type of transaction. Physical gold is classified as a "collectible" by the IRS: the long-term gain (holding period over one year) is taxed up to 28% at the federal level, versus a generally lower rate for stocks. Taxation also varies by state of residence.
exportPoints: Declaration mandatory (FinCEN Form 105) above $10,000 in cash
No specific export authorization for gold for private use
Check your state's sales tax rules
reventePoints: Short/long-term distinction based on holding period (1 year)
Maximum federal rate of 28% on long-term gains (collectible)
Taxation varies by state of residence

faqs.q: Is physical gold treated like a stock for tax purposes?
faqs.a: No, the IRS classifies it as a collectible, with a maximum federal rate of 28% on long-term gain.

faqs.q: Is there a federal VAT on gold in the US?
faqs.a: No, but some states apply a sales tax depending on the transaction.
